title: Police looking to ID man after stabbing outside South Boston dive bar
author: John Waller
bullets: Police are seeking to identify a man after a stabbing occurred outside Croke Park, South Boston, early Sunday morning.
A victim with multiple stab wounds was found following a fight involving several males; the suspects fled toward C Street.
The victim was hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries; police urge anyone with information to contact detectives at 617-343-4742.

title: Yankee Candle founder’s estate sells for far below asking price, will become ‘luxury getaway’
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Yankee Candle founder Michael J. Kittredge II's estate in Leverett, MA, sold for $4.55 million, well below the $23 million asking price.
David and Kerry Reasoner purchased the estate and adjacent property to develop it into a luxury getaway and event space.
The sale ends plans to develop the estate into housing for those 55 and over, previously proposed as Pioneer Pointe.

title: Patriots’ Will Campbell recently got engaged
author: John Waller
bullets: Patriots offensive tackle Will Campbell recently got engaged to Ashlynn Nussmeier in Dallas.
Nussmeier announced the engagement on Instagram, mentioning her excitement to start life with Campbell.
Nussmeier is the daughter of Saints' offensive coordinator Doug Nussmeier and was introduced to Campbell by her brother.

title: Six MassDOT employees resign amid allegations of overtime fraud
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Six MassDOT employees resigned amid allegations of overtime fraud linked to a Charlestown facility.
Employees made over $100,000 in overtime, one increasing pay from $84,000 to $240,000 in a year.
MassDOT is pursuing recovery of improperly obtained compensation and continues the investigation.

title: AI may be deciding whether you get a job interview. Here’s what to know.
author: Pattie Hunt Sinacole
bullets: AI is transforming hiring, handling tasks like resume screening and initial interviews for about 30% of employers.
AI tools streamline candidate selection, but raise concerns about privacy, bias, and assessment of soft skills.
Candidates use AI to prepare resumes and interviews, causing employers to scrutinize the authenticity of applications.

title: Bazooka wielder reported to police was actually carrying a leaf blower
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Bridgewater police responded to a report of a man with a bazooka, which turned out to be a landscaper with a leaf blower.
The report came on May 23 from South and Pleasant streets, a busy area where police received only one call.
Police encourage reporting unusual activity, stressing that it's better to be safe despite potential misunderstandings.

title: Massachusetts has always been a soccer state
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Massachusetts has a storied soccer history, with early teams like Fall River Marksmen dominating the national scene in the 1920s.
The area has produced notable players such as World Cup goal scorer Bert Patenaude and women's national team star Sam Mewis.
Soccer's popularity in Massachusetts was reinforced by events like the 1994 World Cup, setting the stage for the growth of Major League Soccer.

title: A.J. Brown discussed early Patriots fandom, and what Mike Vrabel said to him after his trade from the Eagles
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: A.J. Brown, traded to the Patriots from the Eagles, has been a longtime New England fan, even owning a Patriots rug.
Brown, once disappointed New England didn't draft him in 2019, described his trade to the Patriots as a 'dream come true.'
Mike Vrabel, coach for both the Titans and now the Patriots, welcomed Brown to New England with a familiar directive: 'Get open and catch the ball.'

title: Support pours in for Stoneham teen barred from singing outside shopping plaza
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Brandon Fiore, 17, was barred from singing karaoke outside Stoneham's Redstone Shopping Center after performing there for two years.
Supporters expressed disappointment on Facebook, urging WS Development to allow Fiore to return while praising his talent.
Fiore relocates performances to Stoneham's town common gazebo with a permit, sharing updates on his schedule online.

title: 5 juveniles arrested after police shooting, car chase in Medford
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Five juveniles were arrested after a car break-in led to a police shooting and chase in Medford.
The suspects crashed into a State Police cruiser in Malden and were apprehended after fleeing on foot.
Three officers and one youth sustained minor injuries and were hospitalized following the incident.
